setting.note: Gilded Age Manhattan, where an address is a verdict. Wharton is exact about which streets are respectable and which are not: the shabby old opera house on Fourteenth Street, the chocolate-coloured brownstones, and the bold new houses creeping up towards the Park. Half of these buildings are gone.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Edith Wharton, first published 1920; the author died in 1937.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States (published 1920), and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1937).
rights.attribution: Edith Wharton, The Age of Innocence (1920). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: the shabby red and gold boxes
palette.note: The red of the shabby red-and-gold boxes at the old Academy of Music, where the novel opens. A colour drawn straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.
